items.title: feat(www): ship bash one-liner installer at stash.ac/install
items.content:
## Summary

Phase 2 of \`plans/installer-one-liner.md\`. Ships \`https://stash.ac/install\` as a single-paste bash installer that mirrors how \`fly\` / \`bun\` / \`opencode\` ship.

\`\`\`bash
curl -fsSL https://stash.ac/install | bash
\`\`\`

The script:
1. Picks \`pipx\` > \`uv\` > \`pip3 --user\` (whichever's on PATH)
2. Installs or upgrades \`stashai\` via that manager
3. Bails with actionable hints if none of those are installed
4. Verifies \`stash\` is on PATH (catches the macOS \`~/.local/bin\` not-in-PATH gotcha)
5. \`exec\`s \`stash connect\` — the existing questionnaire handles scope, managed-vs-self-host, browser auth, workspace, and Claude Code plugin install (the step 4 added in 0.1.4)

Both \`/install\` and \`/install.sh\` serve the same content (the no-extension URL goes through a Next.js rewrite).

## Test plan

- [x] \`bash -n www/public/install.sh\` syntax-checks
- [x] Local dev: \`curl -sL http://localhost:3100/install\` returns the script body
- [x] \`/install.sh\` and \`/install\` both serve the script
- [x] \`Content-Type: application/x-sh\` (fine for \`curl | bash\`)
- [ ] Reviewer: after merge, run \`curl -fsSL https://stash.ac/install | bash\` on a fresh box

## Followups

- Phase 3: swap landing-page hero CTA from the agent-prompt-paste to the one-liner
- Codex / Cursor / Gemini / opencode / openclaw plugin auto-install (currently only Claude Code is wired in step 4)

items.title: feat: personal agent notifications 😈
items.content:
## Summary

Adds three owner-toggleable notification preferences to personal agents, widens opportunity pickup to surface `draft` opps to counterparties, and removes the unused agent webhook transport.

Design: [docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-17-personal-agent-notifications-design.md](docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-17-personal-agent-notifications-design.md)

## New Features

- **Notification toggles on `agents`** (`notify_on_opportunity`, `daily_summary_enabled`, `handle_negotiations`, `last_daily_summary_at`). Personal-agent only; surfaced on `GET /agents[/:id]` and writable via `PATCH /agents/:id`.
- **`handle_negotiations` toggle (ALPHA)** grants/revokes `manage:negotiations` on the owner's permission row via transactional `AgentService.reconcileNegotiationsPermission`. Default `false` — explicit opt-in.
- **Daily summary pickup contract** scaffolded in the OpenClaw skill template (`{ kind: 'daily_summary', payload: ... }`). Worker implementation is intentionally deferred until IND-233 clarifies "surfaced" under the refined status vocabulary.
- **Frontend:** Reusable `AlphaBadge` component and a Notifications section on the agent detail page with three toggles (ALPHA badge next to "Handle negotiations on my behalf").

## Behavior Changes

- **Opportunity pickup widened.** `OpportunityDeliveryService.pickupPending` now returns both `pending` and `draft` opportunities. For `draft`, the initiator (identified via `detection.createdBy`) is excluded so they don't see their own chat-created opp on their personal agent — only the counterparty does. Pickup is gated on `agents.notify_on_opportunity = true`; muted agents get empty results. `opportunity_deliveries.delivered_at_status` now records the actual opp status, keeping per-status uniqueness buckets.
- **Personal agent defaults.** New `PERSONAL_AGENT_DEFAULT_ACTIONS` excludes `manage:negotiations`. New personal agents created via `AgentService.create` and seeded via `db-seed` receive five actions (profile, intents, ne

items.title: OpenClaw fleet: identities + corpus wiring + skills + deploy scripts + tests
items.content:
## Summary

Closes the bulk of the Agent Team Linear project's open tickets by wiring the 11 OpenClaw agents to their role-specific corpus of best practices, adding shared + Conductor-specific skills, a unified docker-compose reference, deploy + weekly-refresh scripts, and a comprehensive test suite.

Brings the full scaffolding branch (`agent/tech-writer/scaffold-identity-files` — TEAM.md, USER.md, 11 IDENTITY.md, migrations) to main at the same time.

## Tickets addressed

- **CAR-318** Write TEAM.md — merged from scaffold branch
- **CAR-319** Write 11 IDENTITY.md — merged from scaffold + updated with `## Knowledge corpus` section
- **CAR-320** Write USER.md — merged from scaffold branch
- **CAR-321** Deploy 6 new + rename 11 OpenClaw — 11 named instances deployed on VPS, 5 randoms archived
- **CAR-323** Wire Ollama + Anthropic on all 11 — `OLLAMA_HOST=https://ollama.com` added on every instance
- **CAR-325** Conductor skills — `linear-read`, `paperclip-create`, `app-config-load`
- **CAR-326** Shared skills — `memory-read`, `slack-post-hybrid`, `notion-read`, `langfuse-trace`
- **CAR-328** Unified docker-compose — 22 services, 729 lines, ports 47810–47820
- **CAR-329** E2E test groundwork — 43 bats + pytest tests pass; on-VPS smoke test checks containers up, corpus present, cron scheduled

## What's new in this branch

### `identities/*.md` (11 files)
Each identity now has a `## Knowledge corpus` section pointing to `corpus/<role>/` with 7 expert knowledge files. Mandates session-start skim + weekly deep-study. Cross-references shared seeds (Chip Huyen, Kent C. Dodds, Josh Comeau).

### `skills/`
- `skills/shared/` — 4 skills installed on every agent
- `skills/conductor/` — 3 skills installed only on the Conductor

### `deploy/docker-compose.yml`
Full 22-service unified reference compose. 11 role-based openclaw-* with YAML anchors + per-role overrides.

### `scripts/`
- `deploy-identities.sh` — idempotent rsync from repo to VPS; `--dry-run`, `--role`, `--no-restar

items.title: perf(async): 消除主进程事件循环阻塞（5 commits, T1-T5）
items.content:
## Summary

三个前独立进程（main / memory / agent）合并成单进程共享 event loop 后，高热路径上的同步阻塞会直接卡心跳和 WebSocket。这个 PR 分 5 步完成从地基到全链路的异步化：

- **T1** 地基+紧急止血：`utils/file_utils.py` 新增 `atomic_write_*_async` / `read_json_async`；`main_server.py` 加 `loop.slow_callback_duration = 0.05`；`config_manager.py` characters 加内存缓存 + mtime check；角色删除走 `to_thread(shutil.rmtree)`；`agent_server.py` plugin name 查询换 `httpx.AsyncClient` + `asyncio.Lock`。
- **T2** memory 模块全链路异步化：`recent.py` / `facts.py` / `reflection.py` / `persona.py` 每个 sync IO 都加 `a*` 镜像；`reflection.save_reflections` 的 4 次 fsync 合为 1 次；`memory_server.py` 全部 handler 切到 async 版本。
- **T3** timeindex 异步镜像 + 懒加载：`memory/timeindex.py` 构造器停用"每个角色初始化时预热 engine"循环，改成 `_aensure_engine_exists` 懒加载；新增 9 个 `a*` 方法（`astore_conversation`、`asearch_facts`、`aindex_fact`…）；清掉 T2 放在 `facts.py` 里的 TODO(T3) 占位。
- **T4** agent 路径：`brain/task_executor.py` quota check 改 async、BM25 `stage1_filter` 走 `to_thread`、三个 adapter 的 `is_available()` offload；`openclaw_adapter` / `openfang_adapter` / `browser_use_adapter` / `deduper` 内部同步 IO 在 async 路径里 offload。
- **T5** 收尾：`characters_router.py` 9 处 `save_characters` → `asave_characters`；`jukebox_router` `JukeboxConfig` 加 `asave()` 并迁移 14 个 call site；`workshop_router.perform_cleanup` 在 Steamworks 回调线程里保留 sync 并加说明注释；`.agent/rules/neko-guide.md` 补了一节「单进程 + 事件循环零阻塞」约束。

## 验证

- `uv run ruff check .` 通过（含 PR #842 的 ASYNC210/220/221/222/251 规则）。
- `uv run scripts/check_async_blocking.py` 通过（Thread.join(timeout=) / queue.Queue.get() / socket.recv 的 AST 兜底）。
- 完整 pytest：292 passed、7 failed，失败全是 Playwright 的 headless 兜底（`test_e2e_full_flow` / `test_api_settings` / `test_chara_settings` / `test_memory_browser` / `test_avatar_reaction_bubble`）+ 一个 `test_restricted_providers`，在 origin/main 上同样失败，非本 PR 引入。
- `tests/unit/test_characters_router_model_settings.py` 6 个用例全部通过（T5 需要把 `DummyConfigManager` 补一个 `asave_characters` 的 await-友好 mock）。

## Test plan

- [x] `uv run ruff check .`
- [x] `uv run scripts/check_async_blocking.py`
- [x]

items.title: Raise default LLM idle timeout from 120s to 240s
items.content:
<!-- mesa-description-start -->
## Summary

Bump `DEFAULT_LLM_IDLE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` from 120 to 240 in `src/config/agent-timeout-defaults.ts`.

## Why

On our live OpenClaw box (54.205.14.181), a Slack turn on a session with a large accumulated context hit the 120s idle watchdog before `gpt-5.4` emitted its first streaming token, so the user saw:

> Request timed out before a response was generated. Please try again, or increase `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds` in your config.

Gateway log confirming the 120s idle fallback firing:
```
[agent/embedded] embedded run timeout: runId=bf44dfff... sessionId=f1e92a6a... timeoutMs=120000
[agent/embedded] Profile openai-codex:default timed out. Trying next account...
[agent/embedded] embedded run failover decision: ... decision=surface_error reason=timeout from=openai-codex/gpt-5.4
```

With reasoning-heavy models and large histories, the first token can legitimately take longer than two minutes. 240s is a more forgiving default while still protecting against stuck streams. Users who want different behavior can still override via `agents.defaults.llm.idleTimeoutSeconds`.

## Changes

- `src/config/agent-timeout-defaults.ts` — constant 120 → 240
- `src/config/schema.base.generated.ts` — regenerated (description interpolates the constant)
- `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256` — regenerated

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts` (23 passed)
- [x] `pnpm test src/config/schema.base.generated.test.ts` (5 passed)
- [x] Set equivalent config (`agents.defaults.llm.idleTimeoutSeconds=240`) on the live box and restarted the gateway for immediate user relief

<sup>_Description generated by Mesa. [Update settings](https://wrangler.app.mesa.dev/decisional/settings/pull-requests)_</sup>
<!-- mesa-description-end -->

items.title: feat(cli): connect step 4 — auto-install Claude Code plugin
items.content:
## Summary

Phase 1 of the bash one-liner installer plan (\`plans/installer-one-liner.md\`). Adds **Step 4** to the \`stash connect\` questionnaire: after the workspace step, detect whether Claude Code is installed (\`shutil.which("claude")\`) and offer to install the stash plugin via the official marketplace.

- Default Yes
- Idempotent (re-runs print "already added/installed")
- Silently skipped when no supported agent is detected
- Surfaces the install command's success line so the user sees what happened

Bumps to 0.1.4.

## Why this scope

Plan calls out per-agent install for Claude / Codex / Cursor / Gemini / opencode / openclaw. Only Claude Code has an official marketplace install; the others require bundled hooks files (\`envsubst\` from \`plugins/<agent>/hooks.json\` into \`~/.<agent>/\`), which means stashai needs to ship those files as package data + know each agent's config dir. Tractable but bigger — better as a focused followup.

## Test plan

- [x] \`from cli.main import _detect_claude_code, _install_claude_plugin\` — module imports cleanly
- [x] \`_detect_claude_code()\` returns True on a machine with \`claude\` on PATH
- [ ] Reviewer: smoke a fresh \`stash connect\` after merge — Step 4 prompts and installs cleanly

## Followup

- Phase 2: ship \`https://stash.ac/install\` bash wrapper (handoff to \`stash connect\`)
- Phase 3: landing page swap to lead with one-liner
- Other agents: bundle plugin files into stashai (\`pyproject.toml\` package_data) + add per-agent install logic mirroring \`_install_claude_plugin\`

items.title: Top-tier upgrade: Architecture primer, Migration guide, Gotchas/FAQ, themed README, contributor tooling
items.content:
## Summary

Second pass toward "THE BEST GUIDE ON GITHUB." Builds on #3 with primer/navigation/tooling work: three new flagship parts, a full README hero rebuild, and a CI-backed contribution workflow so the guide stays clean as it grows.

## Type of change
- [x] New part (x3)
- [x] Correction to existing parts
- [x] Tooling / CI / meta

## What's new

**Primers & references (new parts)**
- **Part 25 — Architecture Overview (v4.0+)** — 15-minute primer mapping the five components (gateway, agent runtime, memory layer, skills, surfaces) with a mermaid diagram and a request-flow sequence diagram. Version timeline table so readers can tell which era a piece of advice belongs to.
- **Part 26 — Migration Guide** — opinionated upgrade paths: v3.x → v4.0 → v2026.3.31-beta.1 → v2026.4.x → v2026.4.15-beta.1. Each path has a breaking-change list, steps, known-bad gotchas, and a rollback plan. "Before you upgrade" snapshot script at the top.
- **Part 27 — Gotchas & FAQ** — symptom-indexed troubleshooting ("memory search returns nothing", "gateway keeps restarting", "compaction crashes in a loop", etc.) with cause → fix tables. FAQ section for newcomer questions.

**README rebuild**
- Badges row (version, parts, license, PRs-welcome)
- **Who This Is For** section (four reader archetypes → where to start)
- **Start Here (in this order)** — Part 25 → Part 14 → Part 17 → Part 27
- **What You Get** numbers table (context size, search latency, compaction crash rate, token reduction, audit-trail coverage)
- **What Changed In This Release** — highlights for the 2026.4.15-beta.1 refresh
- **How The Pieces Fit Together** mermaid diagram
- **Navigate By Goal** table (9 goals → starting points), plus the original numeric TOC regrouped into themed sections (Speed / Memory / Orchestration / Knowledge Graph / Hardening / Observability)

**Part 16 deprecation**
- Clear `⚠️ DEPRECATED` banner at the top pointing to Part 22 (memory-core built-in dreaming)
- Kept as archival reference; noted it

items.title: feat(settings): add command palette launcher toggles
items.content:
## What
Adds separate Appearance settings to control the two command palette launchers introduced for compact/mobile layouts:
- the top-bar `Commands` button
- the floating compact-layout `Commands` button

This branch also includes the original mobile command palette launcher work and the follow-up positioning and review-driven accessibility fixes.

## Why
The mobile/compact command palette entrypoints are useful, but operators currently cannot choose to keep one launcher while hiding the other. Some deployments want the top-bar entry only, while others prefer the floating launcher in compact layouts.

Closes #290

## How
- adds two persisted booleans in `SettingsContext`
- adds two independent switches in `AppearanceSettings`
- gates the top-bar Commands trigger with one toggle
- gates the floating compact-layout launcher with the other
- keeps existing command palette behavior otherwise unchanged
- incorporates review-driven fixes:
  - remove the `sm:hidden` dead zone on the floating FAB
  - hide the top-bar `Commands` label on smaller screens
  - support `aria-labelledby` in `Switch` and use it for the new toggles
- includes focused regression tests for the new toggle behavior and accessibility wiring

## Type of Change
- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [x] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change)
- [ ] 📝 Documentation update
- [ ] 🔧 Refactor / chore (no functional change)

## Checklist
- [x] `npm run lint` passes
- [x] `npm run build && npm run build:server` succeeds
- [x] `npm test -- --run` passes
- [x] New features include tests
- [ ] UI changes include a screenshot or screen recording

items.title: fix(codex): prevent gateway crash when app-server subprocess terminates abruptly
items.content:
### Summary

- **Problem**: When the `codex-acp` subprocess terminates abruptly (e.g. misconfigured binary, interactive prompt on stdout, deprecated config error), `CodexAppServerClient.writeMessage()` at `extensions/codex/src/app-server/client.ts:215` writes a JSON-RPC payload to the dead subprocess's `stdin`. Node.js emits an asynchronous `write EPIPE` error on the stream, and because no `error` event handler is registered on `child.stdin`, the error propagates as an unhandled exception that crashes the entire OpenClaw gateway daemon — taking down all connected channels.

- **Root Cause**: Three compounding defects in `CodexAppServerClient`:
  1. **No `error` handler on `child.stdin`** (constructor, line 57–77): the constructor registers `error`/`exit` handlers on the transport (`child`) itself, but never on `child.stdin`. When the pipe breaks, the stdin stream emits an `error` event that has no listener, causing Node.js to throw it as an uncaught exception.
  2. **`writeMessage()` does not check `this.closed`** (line 214–216): even after the client is marked closed by `closeWithError()`, async code paths like `handleServerRequest()` can still resume and call `writeMessage()`, triggering writes to a dead pipe.
  3. **`closeWithError()` performs incomplete cleanup** (line 298–304): unlike `close()` which closes the readline interface and shuts down the transport, `closeWithError()` only sets `closed = true` and rejects pending requests — leaving the readline active (processing more lines → more writes) and the transport open.

- **Fix**: Three targeted changes that address each root cause:
  1. Attach an `error` event handler on `child.stdin` in the constructor that routes errors through `closeWithError()`, preventing unhandled exceptions.
  2. Guard `writeMessage()` with a `this.closed` check, preventing writes to dead pipes from async continuations.
  3. Align `closeWithError()` cleanup with `close()`: close the readline interface and shut down the tr

items.title: fix(codex): attach stdin error listener to prevent gateway EPIPE crash
items.content:
## Summary

Fixes #67886 — attaches a stdin error listener to `CodexAppServerClient` so the codex app-server subprocess can fail cleanly without taking down the whole OpenClaw gateway daemon.

## Root cause

`CodexAppServerTransport.stdin` is a Node `Writable` stream. When the subprocess exits after emitting unparseable stdout (for example an interactive prompt on init, or a deprecated-config error), OpenClaw still tries to write the JSON-RPC initialize payload to the now-closed pipe. Node emits an asynchronous `error` event on that stream; with no listener attached, the default handler rethrows, crashing the entire gateway daemon.

The existing constructor already wires `stdout` / `stderr` / `error` / `exit` handlers on the child, but never wires anything on `stdin`. This PR adds a debug-level listener so the pipe teardown noise is absorbed while the existing `exit` handler continues to run `closeWithError`.

## Changes

- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/client.ts` — attach `child.stdin.on('error', …)` in the constructor, logging at debug level.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/transport.ts` — widen the typed `stdin` shape to expose an optional `on('error', …)` method so tests and alternate transports participate without casting.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/client.test.ts` — assert that emitting `EPIPE` on the harness stdin does not throw.

## Test plan

- [x] `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 npx tsc --noEmit` — no new TS errors on touched files (baseline 243 on main, 243 on branch).
- [x] `pnpm exec oxlint <touched files>` — 0 warnings, 0 errors.
- [x] Added unit test `swallows EPIPE errors emitted on stdin after subprocess exit`.

Local full vitest run is blocked by pre-existing infra drift on my machine (`test/non-isolated-runner.ts` fails to resolve base `TestRunner` import) — reproduces identically on `main`, so it is not caused by this change. CI should exercise the new test normally.

## OpenClaw 项目深度报告

# OpenClaw 项目日报 - 2026年04月17日

## 1. 今日速览

OpenClaw 项目今日保持极高的活跃度，过去24小时内 Issues 与 PR 更新量均达到 500 条，显示出社区与开发团队紧密的互动节奏。项目发布了 `v2026.4.15` 正式版与 Beta 版，重点集成了 Claude Opus 4.7 的图像理解能力及 Google Gemini 的 TTS 支持。然而，新版本伴随的回归问题较多，尤其是 `onboard` 流程崩溃和 Windows UI 异常成为用户反馈焦点。总体而言，项目处于功能快速迭代与稳定性修复并行的关键阶段。

## 2. 版本发布

**最新版本：v2026.4.15** (同日发布 Beta.2)
- **Anthropic 增强**：
  - 支持 Claude Opus 4.7 模型的捆绑图像理解。
  - 增加了 `opus` 别名，优化了 Claude CLI 默认配置。
- **Google TTS 支持**：
  - 在捆绑的 `google` 插件中新增 Gemini 文本转语音支持，包括提供商注册、语音选择及 WAV 格式输出。

## 3. 项目进展

今日共有 **196 个 PR 合并/关闭**，主要进展集中在稳定性修复与新平台支持：

- **平台支持扩展**：PR [#66898](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/66898) 提交了 Windows 原生封装与加固安装程序，旨在解决长期缺失的 Windows 原生体验。
- **关键修复**：
  - **Onboarding 崩溃**：针对近期频发的 `trim` 相关 TypeError，PR [#67736](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/67736) 和 [#67895](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/67895) 增加了类型守卫，修复了用户初始化流程中的阻断性错误。
  - **OpenAI Codex 集成**：PR [#67918](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/67918) 统一了 Responses API 存储能力检测，修复了 OAuth 令牌相关问题。
- **功能增强**：PR [#65554](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/65554) 大幅更新了 WebChat 的媒体支持，增加了对多种图片和音

items.title: [2026-04-16] Google broke its promise to me – now ICE has my data
items.content:
# Hacker News
Google broke its promise to me – now ICE has my data ## 谷歌违反承诺，向执法部门提供用户数据：以Amandla Thomas-Johnson为例 (Google Broke Its Promise to Hand Over User Data to Law Enforcement: The Case of Amandla Thomas-Johnson) 以下是对原文的总结： **事件概述:** 2024年9月，Ph.D. 候选人Amandla Thomas-Johnson 在美国因参加为时五分钟的亲巴勒斯坦抗议活动而受到美国移民及海关执法局 (ICE) 的关注。2025年4月，ICE 向谷歌发送行政传票，要求提供Thomas-Johnson 的数据。随后，谷歌在五月份，在没有给予Thomas-Johnson 任何提出异议机会的情况下，将他的信息提供给了ICE，违反了过去近十年的承诺，即在将用户数据交给执法部门之前通知用户。 **Thomas-Johnson 的经历:** * **抗议活动与躲藏:** 在参加抗议活动后，Thomas-Johnson 受到特朗普政府的关注，并被迫躲藏了三个月。联邦特工在其家中搜寻，一位朋友在坦帕机场被拘留并被询问其下落。 * **数据泄露:** Thomas-Johnson 在日内瓦，瑞士收到来自谷歌的邮件，得知其账户数据已被提供给美国国土安全部。他原本期望像他的朋友 Momodou Taal 一样，在数据被提供之前收到通知并有机会提出异议，但谷歌直接提供了数据，没有提供任何挑战的机会。 * **数据内容与影响:** 传票主要要求提供订阅者信息，包括IP地址、物理地址、会话时间和持续时间等。这些信息组合起来形成了一个详细的监控画像，可能被用于推断其位置、生活习惯和社交联系。Thomas-Johnson 因此感到受到联邦政府调查的威胁，担心其安全、旅行和未来职业生涯。 **谷歌的承诺与例外:** 谷歌承诺在将用户数据交给执法部门之前会通知用户，以便用户有机会提出异议。然而，谷歌存在一些例外情况（例如收到法院的禁令）。Thomas-Johnson 的案件不属于这些例外。尽管ICE 要求谷歌不要通知Thomas-Johnson，但此要求并非法院强制执行或要求。 **法律行动:** 电子边疆基金会 (EFF) 已向加利福尼亚州和纽约州检察长办公室提交投诉，指控谷歌违反了欺诈性商业行为，破坏了其对用户的承诺。 **核心问题:** * 科技公司（如谷歌）拥有大量用户数据，这使得执法部门能够进行大规模调查。 * 政府与科技公司合作，结合国家力量、公司数据和算法推理，可能导致难以察觉和难以挑战的监控。 * 任何个人都可能成为执法部门的目标，个人隐私面临威胁。 **Thomas-Johnson 的现状:** Thomas-Johnson 目前已离开美国，但仍然感到受到美国政府的潜在影响。 他质疑谁应该为发生在他身上的事情负责。 **更新:** 文章更新补充了谷歌对通知政策的例外情况，并且这些例外情况并不适用于Thomas-Johnson的案件。
[eff.org](https://www.eff.org/deeplinks/2026/04/google-broke-its-promise-me-now-ice-has-my-data) | 1216 points by [Brajeshwar](https://news.ycombinator.com/user?id=Brajeshwar) at 01:44:26 | [536 comments](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47782570)
The local LLM ecosystem doesn’t need Ollama ## 总结：为什么你应该放弃 Ollama 本文批评了 Ollama 项目，认为它在本地 LLM 领域占据主导地位并非因为技术优势，而是因为它最早提供了易于使用的 `llama.cpp` 包装。文章指出，Ollama 在发展过程中系统性地隐藏其技术来源，误导用户，并偏离了最初的本地优先使命，而这一切都与获得风险投资有关。 以下是文章的主要观点： *

items.title: feat: Make channel startup non-blocking with graceful degradation
items.author: rafiki270

items.title: fix: Enable unknown-tool stream guard by default to fail fast on hallucinated tools
items.content:
## upstream

OpenClaw v2026.4.15 release — fix to `tools.loopDetection.unknownToolThreshold` behavior (referenced in release notes under "Agents/tool-loop" fix)

## what it does

Previously, the unknown-tool stream guard required `tools.loopDetection.enabled: true` to be explicitly set. Without it, `resolveUnknownToolGuardThreshold()` returned undefined and the protection was disabled.

The fix enables the guard **by default** — it now triggers whenever a tool is called that is not registered in the current run (objectively not found), regardless of the `tools.loopDetection.enabled` setting.

The guard triggers a "Tool X not found" loop with a configurable threshold (default: 10 attempts) then surfaces an error instead of allowing infinite retry loops.

## why it fits nessie

In a multi-agent Orchestrator→Worker architecture, the Orchestrator may present tools to the LLM that the Worker's session does not have registered (due to different skill configurations, or schema mismatches across provider boundaries). Without this guard, a hallucinated tool call loops "Tool X not found" until the embedded-run timeout — wasting tokens and creating confusing failures.

**High relevance for:** MiniMax model provider compatibility layer (schema mismatches between OpenAI-style tool schemas and MiniMax's expected format).

## suggested implementation

The fix is primarily about changing default behavior:

```typescript
// Before (vulnerable):
const threshold = tools.loopDetection.enabled
  ? tools.loopDetection.unknownToolThreshold
  : undefined;  // protection OFF by default!

// After (secure):
const threshold = tools.loopDetection.unknownToolThreshold ?? 10;  // default 10

// If threshold is met, fail fast with:
throw new FatalToolError(\`Tool \"\$NAME\" is not registered in this session. Too many retry attempts (\${count}).\`);
```

In practice: audit the `resolveUnknownToolGuardThreshold()` call site and ensure it no longer defers to `tools.loopDetection.enabled`.

items.author: rafiki270

items.title: Gateway crashes on ENETUNREACH — uncaught network exception kills entire process
items.content:
## Summary

The gateway process exits with code 1 when a single outbound connection hits `ENETUNREACH`. A momentary network unreachability (e.g. Telegram's IP briefly unavailable) causes the entire gateway to crash, disconnecting all agents and sessions.

## Error from log

```
2026-04-16T18:44:37.946+07:00 [openclaw] Uncaught exception: Error: connect ENETUNREACH 149.154.167.220:443 - Local (0.0.0.0:62855)
    at internalConnect (node:net:1110:16)
    ...
    at connect (/Users/greg/codebase/openclaw/node_modules/undici/lib/core/connect.js:70:20)
ELIFECYCLE  Command failed with exit code 1.
```

`149.154.167.220` is a Telegram API IP. The connection error bubbled up as an uncaught exception and terminated the Node process.

## Impact

- All TUI clients show "gateway disconnected: closed | idle"
- All agent sessions drop — "not connected to gateway — message not sent"
- Gateway must be manually restarted
- Any in-progress agent work is lost

## Expected behavior

A failed outbound network connection (even a persistent one) should be caught and handled gracefully — log the error, back off and retry, and keep the gateway running. A single provider's network being temporarily unreachable should never take down the whole process.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Run gateway with Telegram connected
2. Briefly interrupt network connectivity to Telegram's IPs (or have ISP/routing hiccup)
3. Gateway exits immediately

## Suggested fix

- Wrap the Telegram connection/polling loop in a try/catch or attach an `error` event handler on the underlying socket/agent so `ENETUNREACH` is caught at the connection level
- Add a top-level `process.on('uncaughtException')` handler that logs the error and decides whether it is fatal rather than always crashing
- Alternatively, wrap outbound requests in a circuit breaker that absorbs transient network errors

items.title: BUG: opencode-go always marked unavailable when system is idle
items.content:
## Problem

`minimax-quota-check.sh` marks `opencode_go_available=false` even though `provider-status.json` shows opencode-go as "ok:0%" with no errors and the openclaw auth state shows 0 errorCount.

## Root Cause

The `check_opencode_go_quota()` function in `minimax-quota-check.sh` determines availability by checking if there have been >5 assistant messages from opencode-go in the last hour:

```bash
check_opencode_go_quota() {
  local db_path="${HOME_DIR}/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db"
  if [[ ! -f "$db_path" ]]; then
    return 1
  fi
  local recent_calls
  recent_calls="$(sqlite3 "$db_path" "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM message WHERE time_updated >= $last_hour_ms AND json_extract(data, $.providerID) = opencode-go AND json_extract(data, $.role) = assistant" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  if [[ "${recent_calls:-0}" -gt 5 ]]; then
    echo "available"
    return 0
  fi
  return 1
}
```

This conflates "has recent activity" with "is available for use". When the system has been idle (e.g., during Claude rate limit cooldown), there are 0 recent calls, so opencode-go is incorrectly marked unavailable.

Additionally, when `sqlite3` is not installed on the system, the query silently fails and the function always returns 1 (unavailable). This was the case on this VM until just now.

## Impact

- opencode-go is not considered as a fallback provider when minimax/deepseek are unavailable
- The `recommended_provider` never switches to opencode-go even when it is healthy
- Misleading health state in minimax-quota.json

## Suggested Fix

Replace or supplement the activity-based check with a proper provider health/budget check:

1. Check `provider-status.json` opencode-go status (ok vs disabled)
2. Check openclaw auth state `usageStats["opencode-go:default"]` for disabledUntil and errorCount
3. Only fall back to the recent-activity heuristic if the above checks are inconclusive

Also ensure `sqlite3` is in the system dependencies.

## Environment

- VM: vm-orb-shell
- Detected: 2026-04-

## Análise aprofundada do projeto principal

# Relatório do Projeto NullClaw — 2026-04-17

---

## 1. Panorama do Dia

O projeto NullClaw registrou **alta atividade** nas últimas 24h, com 27 eventos combinados (13 issues e 14 PRs). **Nenhum lançamento** foi publicado, mas **11 PRs foram merged/fechados**, indicando que a equipe está focada em estabilização pós-release. A base de código foi migrada para **Zig 0.16** (PR #823), marcando uma atualização significativa de infraestrutura. Apenas **1 issue permanece aberta** (#826), enquanto o restante foi fechado, sugerindo bom throughput de triagem. A atividade recente concentra-se em **correções de segurança, estabilidade de sandboxes Docker/Linux e melhorias na experiência CLI**.

---

## 2. Lançamentos

**Nenhuma release publicada nas últimas 24h.**

A equipe parece estar em fase de preparação para um próximo release, dado o volume de PRs merged (11) e a migração para Zig 0.16.

---

items.author: cesarnml

items.title: [Bug]: exec tool returns "No result provided" starting in 2026.4.14
items.content:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

exec and process tools return "No result provided" (empty results) starting in 2026.4.14 while all other tools work correctly; rollback to 2026.4.12 resolves the issue.

### Steps to reproduce

npm install -g openclaw@2026.4.14 (or 2026.4.15)
openclaw gateway restart
Open webchat, /new to start a fresh session
Send any message that triggers an exec tool call (e.g. "run whoami")
Observe: tool call fires, system messages show execution with exit code 0, but tool result returns "No result provided" to the agent
npm install -g openclaw@2026.4.12 --force && openclaw gateway restart — exec works again

### Expected behavior

On 2026.4.12 (known-good version), exec tool calls return the full stdout/stderr output and exit code to the agent session. For example, exec whoami returns root with exit code 0 and the agent can read and act on the result. This was confirmed working on 2026.4.12 across all tested models (GLM-5, GLM-5.1, DeepSeek-V3.2) on 4/16/2026.

### Actual behavior

Actual behavior:
On 2026.4.14 and 2026.4.15, exec tool calls dispatch and execute successfully (system messages show commands completing with exit code 0), but the tool result is not returned to the agent session. The agent receives "No result provided" instead of the command output. This causes lossless-claw to detect missing tool results and insert synthetic error repairs, and the agent enters a diagnostic loop attempting to understand why its tools are failing. All other tools (read, write, web_search, web_fetch, cron, memory_search) work normally in the same session.

### OpenClaw version

2026.4.14, 2026.4.15

### Operating system

linux 6.8.0

### Install method

npm install -g openclaw

### Model

Tested across three models on Featherless.ai — zai-org/GLM-5, zai-org/GLM-5.1, and deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3.2. Bug reproduces on all three. Not model-specific.

### Provider / routing chain

Provider / rout

items.title: Is it possible to use local AI model provider instead of OpenAI/Anthropic in the UI version?
items.content:
Commenting on something I cannot find anywhere. I would like to simply use my local AI model in the UI not the terminal. The specific AI model I wanna use in LM studio doesn't show up only qwen and GPT OSS. Plus it's still in the terminal. 

No I am not scared of the terminal I use it often for simple commands but it's not pretty like the nice UI where I can chat with the AI like codex. In the UI there should be the option to hook up a local model via an open AI compatible API which LM studio provides. 

I understand development time, so I understand if this isn't implemented I will look for alternatives but I love the UI and the open source version of codex. all I want is codex but instead of having to pay some huge AI company like Open AI I can have a app like codex and just plug in my local AI model from lm studio and work with it like in codex with GPT but locally. 

If this already exists NOT in the terminal but in the UI please tell me bc the AI's I kept asking keep hallucinating telling me to click some engine tab in settings when thats not even a thing. Regardless of if you implement this I do hope this project succeeds because I LOVE the UI and dark mode aesthetic and the face you can choose terminal or UI I just wish I could plug in my local model as easy as it was for auto claw.

## OpenClaw Deep Dive

# OpenClaw Project Digest — 2026-04-17

---

## 1. Today's Overview

OpenClaw remains a **high-velocity, high-activity** project with 500 issues and 500 PRs updated in the last 24 hours. The release cadence is aggressive, with **v2026.4.15** shipping today alongside a beta.2 prerelease. However, the volume of regressions, onboarding crashes, and provider breakages suggests the project is operating near the edge of its stability envelope. The community is deeply engaged—top issues have 80–120 comments—but maintainers face a significant triage burden as Windows, Linux, and multi-provider support expands faster than platform hardening can keep pace.

---

items.title: Theme: Open Claw (navy + crimson + Clash Display/Satoshi)
items.content:
## Summary

Novo tema **Open Claw** disponível em `/settings → Tema`.

### Paleta
- **Fundo**: `#050911` (navy profundo)
- **Texto**: `#F0F4FF` (ice-white)
- **Accent** (títulos, botões, focus): `#FF4D4D` (crimson)

### Tipografia
- **Display** (h1/h2/`.display`/`.display-sm`): **Clash Display** 500/600/700
- **Body**: **Satoshi** 300/400/500/700
- Fontes via Fontshare CSS API (`@import` em `globals.css`) — sem downloads, com `display=swap` pra não bloquear render

### Infra de fontes
- Dois CSS vars globais novos: `--font-body` e `--font-display` no `:root`
- `body` usa `var(--font-body)`; `.display`/`.display-sm` usam `var(--font-display)`
- Cada tema pode sobrescrever ambos (só Open Claw sobrescreve por ora)

### Detalhe Open Claw
- `.display`/`.display-sm` herdam `color: var(--accent)` no tema → todos os títulos e números grandes ficam em crimson, como a pedido

### Arquivos alterados
- `src/app/globals.css` — `@import` Fontshare, vars de fonte, bloco `.theme-openclaw`, override de cor em títulos
- `src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` — novo valor `openclaw` no tipo/lista/applyClass
- `src/app/layout.tsx` — FOUC script reconhece `openclaw`
- `src/app/settings/ThemeSelector.tsx` — swatch + grid 2 colunas (4 temas)

## Test plan

- [ ] `/settings → Tema` mostra 4 cards (Default / Gohan / Beast / Open Claw)
- [ ] Swatch do Open Claw mostra as 4 cores corretas
- [ ] Ativar → toda UI vira navy + títulos em crimson
- [ ] Fonte dos títulos é Clash Display (visível pelo tracking apertado / design geométrico)
- [ ] Fonte do corpo é Satoshi
- [ ] Reload não causa flash — FOUC script aplica tema antes de hidratar
- [ ] Voltar pro Default restaura fontes do sistema
- [ ] CTAs (ex: "Iniciar treino") ficam em fundo crimson com texto navy

items.author: felippecmp

items.title: feat: redesign OpenClaw control plane around CLI and HTTP
items.content:
## Summary
- keep Podman lifecycle and the existing `/claw/*` route surface while replacing the external privileged WebSocket control plane
- move agent administration onto in-container OpenClaw CLI commands and preserve BrowserOS workspace/role bootstrap behavior
- proxy `/v1/chat/completions` streaming back into BrowserOS SSE events with compatible status and session handling

## Design
This PR switches the OpenClaw integration seam from a long-lived host-side operator WebSocket to two narrower container-friendly adapters: a CLI-backed admin client for discrete control-plane operations and an HTTP SSE chat client for `/v1/chat/completions`. `OpenClawService` remains the orchestrator for Podman lifecycle, provider/config management, and BrowserOS-specific bootstrapping, while the extension-facing `/claw/*` routes stay stable.

## Test plan
- `bunx biome check apps/server/src/api/routes/openclaw.ts apps/server/src/api/services/openclaw/openclaw-admin-client.ts apps/server/src/api/services/openclaw/openclaw-http-chat-client.ts apps/server/src/api/services/openclaw/openclaw-service.ts apps/server/src/api/services/openclaw/openclaw-types.ts apps/server/tests/api/routes/openclaw.test.ts apps/server/tests/api/services/openclaw/openclaw-admin-client.test.ts apps/server/tests/api/services/openclaw/openclaw-http-chat-client.test.ts apps/server/tests/api/services/openclaw/openclaw-service.test.ts`
- `bun run --filter @browseros/server typecheck`
- `bun --env-file=.env.development test apps/server/tests/api/routes/openclaw.test.ts apps/server/tests/api/services/openclaw/openclaw-admin-client.test.ts apps/server/tests/api/services/openclaw/openclaw-http-chat-client.test.ts apps/server/tests/api/services/openclaw/openclaw-service.test.ts`

items.title: feat: rebrand to Open Claw + fix Volcengine ASR + hotkey improvements
items.content:
## Summary

- **Rebrand to Open Claw**: package name/productName, tray tooltip/menu, storage dir `~/.feishu-agent` → `~/.openclaw`, remove all Feishu/lark references from agent prompt and memory defaults
- **Fix critical Volcengine ASR bug**: audio packets used `SERIAL_JSON` (0x01) in header byte[2] instead of `SERIAL_RAW` (0x00) — server was rejecting audio frames as malformed JSON
- **ASR improvements**: add `language='zh-CN'` to init request; dynamic `model_name` (`bigmodel_v2` for SeedASR resources); better error messages for 401/403/timeout/missing-config
- **Hotkey**: Right Option short press now opens agent panel (identical to Right Ctrl short press)
- **Code fixes**: remove `require()` calls, dedup imports, replace `uuid` with built-in `crypto.randomUUID()`, fix `tsconfig.json` deprecation warnings
- **CLAUDE.md**: project context, architecture, key files, user preferences, change log

## Test plan

- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` — passes (0 errors)
- [ ] `pnpm lint` — passes (0 errors, 1 pre-existing warning)
- [ ] Launch app: tray shows "Open Claw"
- [ ] Short-press Right Option OR Right Ctrl → agent panel opens
- [ ] Hold Right Option → starts voice recording (floating window shows)
- [ ] Hold Right Ctrl → starts voice recording for agent mode
- [ ] With valid `.env` creds: voice input produces Volcengine transcript
- [ ] Memory data stored in `~/.openclaw/` not `~/.feishu-agent/`

items.author: renovate[bot]

items.title: Integration: PaperClaw (autonomous peer-reviewed paper generator for OpenCode)
items.content:
## Proposal: Add PaperClaw as an optional integration

Hi maintainers 👋 — opening this as an exploratory issue (not a drive-by PR) to ask if a **PaperClaw** integration would be welcome in OpenCode.

### What is PaperClaw

PaperClaw (`npm i -g paperclaw`) is a tiny zero-dependency client that turns any LLM into an autonomous peer-reviewed researcher on the [P2PCLAW](https://www.p2pclaw.com) network. Given a research idea, it:

1. Registers the agent on the P2PCLAW silicon hive (Ed25519 identity)
2. Researches the topic via arXiv + CrossRef
3. Passes an automated IQ/domain tribunal (≥ 60% to clear)
4. Runs code in the sandboxed Lab
5. Publishes a 7-section paper (Abstract → Conclusion + References, Lean 4 proofs, ≥ 8 real DOIs)
6. Receives a calibrated 10-dim score from a panel of 8–10 LLM judges
7. Returns a print-ready PDF (orange-accent template, crab-claw watermark, share-to-X/LinkedIn/Reddit)

It's all public, open endpoints at `https://www.p2pclaw.com/api/*`, MIT-licensed, and the full source is at https://github.com/Agnuxo1/OpenCLAW-P2P.

### Integration drop-in for OpenCode

We already maintain a ready-to-use integration file for OpenCode at:

- [`integrations/opencode/`](https://github.com/Agnuxo1/OpenCLAW-P2P/tree/main/paperclaw/integrations/opencode)

A `paperclaw-rules.md` rules file for OpenCode. Paste into the project's rules dir; OpenCode will learn to call the PaperClaw pipeline when the user asks for a research paper. (The integration is backend-agnostic — works with any OpenCode provider.)

### The ask

We'd love your feedback on the preferred upstream path. Three options, all fine:

1. **Accept a PR** that adds PaperClaw to your docs / examples / plugin index (we open it on request).
2. **Leave as a community integration** — keep it in our repo and add a one-line mention in your integrations page.
3. **Not a fit** — close this issue; we'll keep the integration in our repo for end-users who want it.

Happy to answer anything. Thanks for the great wo

### What is PaperClaw

PaperClaw (`npm i -g paperclaw`) is a tiny zero-dependency client that turns any LLM into an autonomous peer-reviewed researcher on the [P2PCLAW](https://www.p2pclaw.com) network. Given a research idea, it:

1. Registers the agent on the P2PCLAW silicon hive (Ed25519 identity)
2. Researches the topic via arXiv + CrossRef
3. Passes an automated IQ/domain tribunal (≥ 60% to clear)
4. Runs code in the sandboxed Lab
5. Publishes a 7-section paper (Abstract → Conclusion + References, Lean 4 proofs, ≥ 8 real DOIs)
6. Receives a calibrated 10-dim score from a panel of 8–10 LLM judges
7. Returns a print-ready PDF (orange-accent template, crab-claw watermark, share-to-X/LinkedIn/Reddit)

It's all public, open endpoints at `https://www.p2pclaw.com/api/*`, MIT-licensed, and the full source is at https://github.com/Agnuxo1/OpenCLAW-P2P.

### Integration drop-in for Exo

We already maintain a ready-to-use integration file for Exo at:

- [`integrations/exo/`](https://github.com/Agnuxo1/OpenCLAW-P2P/tree/main/paperclaw/integrations/exo)

An Exo cluster config (`paperclaw-cluster.json`) that assigns one node as the PaperClaw orchestrator and the rest as Lab workers. Tested on a 3-node laptop cluster.

### The ask

We'd love your feedback on the preferred upstream path. Three options, all fine:

1. **Accept a PR** that adds PaperClaw to your docs / examples / plugin index (we open it on request).
2. **Leave as a community integration** — keep it in our repo and add a one-line mention in your integrations page.
3. **Not a fit** — close this issue; we'll keep the integration in our repo for end-users who want it.

Happy to answer anything. Thanks for the great work on Exo 🦀

1. **Accept a PR** that adds PaperClaw to your docs / examples / plugin index (we open it on request).
2. **Leave as a community integration** — keep it in our repo and add a one-line mention in your integrations page.

Happy to answer anything. Thanks for the great work on Llamafile 🦀

items.title: Integration: PaperClaw (autonomous peer-reviewed paper generator for LlamaIndex)
items.content:
## Proposal: Add PaperClaw as an optional integration

Hi maintainers 👋 — opening this as an exploratory issue (not a drive-by PR) to ask if a **PaperClaw** integration would be welcome in LlamaIndex.

### What is PaperClaw

PaperClaw (`npm i -g paperclaw`) is a tiny zero-dependency client that turns any LLM into an autonomous peer-reviewed researcher on the [P2PCLAW](https://www.p2pclaw.com) network. Given a research idea, it:

1. Registers the agent on the P2PCLAW silicon hive (Ed25519 identity)
2. Researches the topic via arXiv + CrossRef
3. Passes an automated IQ/domain tribunal (≥ 60% to clear)
4. Runs code in the sandboxed Lab
5. Publishes a 7-section paper (Abstract → Conclusion + References, Lean 4 proofs, ≥ 8 real DOIs)
6. Receives a calibrated 10-dim score from a panel of 8–10 LLM judges
7. Returns a print-ready PDF (orange-accent template, crab-claw watermark, share-to-X/LinkedIn/Reddit)

It's all public, open endpoints at `https://www.p2pclaw.com/api/*`, MIT-licensed, and the full source is at https://github.com/Agnuxo1/OpenCLAW-P2P.

### Integration drop-in for LlamaIndex

We already maintain a ready-to-use integration file for LlamaIndex at:

- [`integrations/llamaindex/`](https://github.com/Agnuxo1/OpenCLAW-P2P/tree/main/paperclaw/integrations/llamaindex)

A native LlamaIndex Tool (`PaperClawTool` in `paperclaw_tool.py`) that exposes the full pipeline (register, research, tribunal, lab, publish, score) as individual tool calls for any LlamaIndex ReActAgent or FunctionAgent. Zero extra deps beyond `requests`.

### The ask

We'd love your feedback on the preferred upstream path. Three options, all fine:

1. **Accept a PR** that adds PaperClaw to your docs / examples / plugin index (we open it on request).
2. **Leave as a community integration** — keep it in our repo and add a one-line mention in your integrations page.
3. **Not a fit** — close this issue; we'll keep the integration in our repo for end-users who want it.

Happy to answer anything. Thanks

items.title: CLAUDE.md: bundle evals with transform skills
items.content:
## Summary

Codifies a new rule in root `CLAUDE.md`: transform skills (chroma-key, resize, transcript scrub, audio strip) must bundle automated quality checks — compute a metric after the transform, print to stderr, exit nonzero on threshold violation under `--strict`.

Prompted by a session in `idvorkin/idvorkin.github.io` where PR #15 (blob raccoon-claw-trio) shipped a translucent Cybertruck because the subagent's chroma-key step had no automated eval on its output — visual inspection in context looked fine, but the alpha channel was wrong. PR #156 subsequently baked a `flood-fill + eval_alpha` pattern into `skills/gen-image`; this rule generalizes that pattern to any transform skill so the next analogous skill (resize, scrub, strip) ships with evals from day one rather than after a silent-failure regression.

Appended to "Report Generators: Measure, Don't Hardcode" rather than a new section — both rules address the same silent-rot failure mode in LLM-generated output (hardcoded footnotes vs unreviewed pixels). Single prose sentence + reference pointer to `skills/gen-image`, matching the file's voice.

## Test plan

- [x] `git diff` limited to two lines in `CLAUDE.md` (+ one prettier italic normalization outside the target line)
- [x] Prettier pre-commit hook passes
- [x] `SKIP=test` used for known-flaky telegram watchdog tests (tracked in chop-conventions #109-adjacent)
- [ ] Reviewer confirms the rule belongs in "Report Generators" vs a new "Evals" section
items.author: idvorkin-ai-tools

items.title: feat(openclaw): switch to full-trust mode (auth.mode=none + HTTP SSE chat)
items.content:
## Summary

- Removes the Ed25519 / device-pairing / signed-handshake WS protocol from the bun sidecar and switches the local OpenClaw gateway to native \`gateway.auth.mode = \"none\"\`. Sidecar now identifies as \`client.id = \"control-ui\"\`, which upstream's \`connect-policy.ts:53\` already handles by skipping pairing for localhost-trust deployments.
- Moves chat streaming from WS (\`sessions.subscribe\` + \`agent\` RPC) to HTTP SSE via \`/v1/chat/completions\`. WS is now used only for agent CRUD (\`agents.list/create/delete\`).
- Deletes all \`signature_expired\` / \`pairing_required\` / \`token_mismatch\` recovery branches. They can't fire anymore. \`gateway-client.ts\` shrank from 754 → ~270 LOC; \`openclaw-service.ts\` from 1141 → ~905 LOC.

## Design

The old design treated a localhost-only sidecar-to-container pair with the same ceremony as a remote operator joining a shared gateway: Ed25519 key pairs, challenge-response, pre-seeded \`paired.json\`, scope grants, token rotation, clock-skew-sensitive signatures. Every one of those moving parts was a failure mode in practice (silent \`missing scope\` under \`--allow-unconfigured\`, \`signature expired\` after Mac sleep, pairing drift on volume reset).

The actual trust model is: same machine, same user, loopback only. The real security boundary is Podman's port mapping (\`127.0.0.1:18789\`). Matching the protocol to the trust model: set \`gateway.auth.mode = \"none\"\`, identify the sidecar as a Control UI operator client (upstream OpenClaw honors that), drop device identity entirely. Chat stays HTTP-based because that's how the rest of BrowserOS already does streaming.

Spec: \`packages/browseros-agent/.llm/superpowers/specs/2026-04-16-openclaw-full-trust-mode-design.md\`
Plan: \`packages/browseros-agent/.llm/superpowers/plans/2026-04-16-openclaw-full-trust-mode.md\`

## Test plan

- [ ] Clean \`~/.browseros/openclaw\` and run \`bun run dev:watch\`; confirm \`/claw/setup\` bootstraps without \`hasDeviceIdent

items.title: chore(migration): SQLite → Postgres 数据 cutover — 彻底重构 4/5
items.content:
## 概述

彻底重构 5 PR 方案的第 4 个：SQLite → Postgres 数据 cutover（**工具 + 文档**；实际数据迁移由主理人在合并后手动执行）。

**PR-a/b/c 已合并**，workers / creator-api 全部走 Postgres，SQLite 侧只剩 1 条"龙虾" topic（`f108b4d8-244e-4663-bcf6-2e7816ab00fe`, status=研究中）。本 PR 负责安全地搬迁这条数据并冻结 SQLite 侧，为 PR-e（清理 + 端到端 CI）铺路。

Brain Task: `477a8fce-f109-4c70-947e-0f1fcc3b048e`

## Scope

| 文件 | 作用 |
|------|------|
| `scripts/migrate-sqlite-to-pg.py` | 一次性迁移脚本（dry-run / apply / backup-first） |
| `services/creator/migrations/999_freeze_sqlite.sql` | 把 SQLite 的 `topics` 表改名并建同名空 view |
| `services/creator/tests/test_migrate_sqlite_to_pg.py` | 10 个单测（含幂等、空 DB、freeze 后重跑） |
| `scripts/MIGRATION_CUTOVER.md` | cutover SOP + 回滚步骤 |

**严格不动**：workers / creator-api / apps/api 业务代码；`pipeline_runs.topic_id` 类型（留给 PR-e）；nginx；无 PR merge。

## 技术决策

- 用 stdlib `sqlite3` + `subprocess` 调 `psql`；不引入 `psycopg2`（creator `.venv` 未装，且一次性使用）
- `ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING` 保证幂等：本地已验证"真跑 → 插入 1 行；重跑 --apply → 跳过 1 行"
- 默认 `--dry-run`，明示 `--apply` 才写；`--backup-first` 先把 SQLite 复制到 `services/creator/data/backups/creator.db.bak-<UTC 时间戳>`（含 -wal/-shm 附属文件）
- `target_platforms` TEXT JSON → Postgres JSONB；UUID `::uuid` cast；空串 / None 归一化为 `NULL`
- 连接参数优先级：CLI args > 环境变量 > `apps/api/.env` > 默认（localhost / cecelia）

## 验证

### 单测
```
pytest services/creator/tests/test_migrate_sqlite_to_pg.py
10 passed in 0.02s
```

相关测试套件（`topics_forwarding` / `topic_worker_http` / `pipeline_worker_http` / `pipeline_worker/`）：**57 passed**，本 PR 无回归。

### 本地真跑（已在 worktree 中执行）

**Dry-run**：
```
[SQLite] topics 总数: 1
[Postgres] zenithjoy.topics 现有: 1
  已存在 id: e5d9d8c0-331f-4dd7-92f9-dde8105ebd4c
[PLAN] 将插入 1 行，跳过 0 行（已存在）
将插入：
  - id=f108b4d8-244e-4663-bcf6-2e7816ab00fe | status=研究中 | title=为什么 2026 年龙虾 让一人公司成为为了可能
DRY-RUN 结束。
```

**Apply + backup-first**：
```
[BACKUP] SQLite 已备份到: services/creator/data/backups/creator.db.bak-20260416-152217
[OK] 已插入: f108b4d8-244e-4663-bcf6-2e7816ab00fe

[VERIFY]
  Postgres topics 迁移前: 1
  Postgres topics 迁移后: 2
  本次插入:
```

### LINUX DO 热门话题（5）
- [用 Claude 要实名了，内地用户怎么办？](https://linux.do/t/topic/1972931) · 2026-04-15 16:54  摘要：Anthropic 正在给 Claude 加上真实证件验证，目前主要针对新注册账号。新号门槛大幅提高，老号价值跟着涨了。 Anthropic 官方帮助中心上线了"Claude 上的身份验证"页面。核心流程：提交护照、驾照或国家身份证，配合摄像头拍实时自拍，大概 5 分钟。不接受复印件、截图、数字证件。 目前是灰度推出，主要针对新注册账号和触发风控的场景。Anthropic 的原话是"当您访问某些功能时"才会看到验证提示。已经在正常使用的老账号，目前没有大规模推送验证的迹象。 接码平台批量注册的路子，成本和难度都在飙升。而且中国护照验证等于主动暴露内地身份
- [我在路上与人口角被打，报警经历。各位老友以此为戒](https://linux.do/t/topic/1973365) · 2026-04-15 17:50  摘要：今天在外面与某电瓶车公婆发生口角，被踢了一脚倒地不起。 对方男甚至逃逸，把他老婆留在原地。我一直抓着嫌疑人亲属（后面知道是老婆），后面到了派出所。 对方承担CT检查费用并且支付谅解费。 事情经过： 对方在窄车道后面一直哔哔我，我行人。后面对方强行骑电瓶车超越，甚至碰到了我。至此无事发生。 然后对方回头吼我走路玩手机。我说：玩手机怎么了，我就玩手机。然后对方下车威胁扬言打我，然后我脱口而出脏话你有本事打我啊（是的，草泥马）。然后矛盾继续升级，对方过来：你是不是找打，打死你啊（大概是这类话。），我继续嘲讽。没想到真的给我来了一脚。遂倒地。对方逃逸。 他亲属（
- [智谱GLM海外版全线涨价最高140%，中外价差超一倍，「老外税」引发社区激辩！](https://linux.do/t/topic/1971401) · 2026-04-15 13:45  摘要：智谱 4 月 11 日对 GLM Coding Plan 海外版全线涨价 80%-140%。Max 月费从 80 美元涨至 160 美元，Pro 从 30 美元涨至 72 美元，Lite 从 10 美元涨至 18 美元（均为含 10% 邀请折扣价）。同一 Max 套餐国内售价 469 元（约 68 美元），涨价后海外版是国内版的两倍多。加上 GLM-5.1 发布同期 API 调价 10%，这已是智谱 2026 年的第三次提价。 海外开发者 Lex 发帖称这种「老外税」太疯狂。 他实测发现，通过微信或支付宝付款即可购买国内版，获取的 API 密钥在海外同样
- [关于claude（sonnet）公益站的一些问题](https://linux.do/t/topic/1974784) · 2026-04-15 21:00  摘要：最近想搞点kiro的free号接claude的haiku和sonnet做公益站，佬们有需求吗 （还有个问题，qwen的公益站还有必要开吗，目前佬的强度都不高，还能承载很多的佬，得加个ip池就好了） 26 个帖子 - 20 位参与者 阅读完整话题

items.title: 🐛 [服务端] /v1/bot/stream/start 接口持续返回 400，Bot 回复被迫 fallback 到 deliver 导致延迟 2 分钟
items.content:
## 问题描述

Bot 发送流式消息时，`/v1/bot/stream/start` 接口持续返回 HTTP 400 错误。每次回复都 fallback 到普通 deliver 模式，导致用户在 Octo 聊天窗口看到 Bot 回复有约 **2 分钟延迟**。

在 OpenClaw Control UI 上可以即时看到 Bot 的回复，但在 Octo 客户端（DMWork 渠道）里要等很久才收到。

## 时间线示例

1. 16:50 用户发消息，Bot 收到
2. 模型生成回复约 60 秒
3. `stream/start` 返回 400，fallback 到 deliver
4. 16:52 消息才到达用户端（延迟约 2 分钟）

## 期望行为

`stream/start` 正常返回，Bot 消息通过流式模式实时推送给用户。

## 实际行为

`stream/start` 一直 400，每条回复都走 fallback deliver，严重影响对话体验。

## 影响

所有通过 Octo 渠道对话的 Bot 都受影响，回复延迟从即时变成 1-2 分钟。

## 截图

龙虾日志分析截图已提供（OpenClaw Control UI 显示消息已生成，但 DMWork 渠道延迟送达）。

## 原始来源

DMWork group:Bug反馈群 user:Wing (uid:318c02819014480c8cd11523f06e4309)
items.author: lejunliu

items.title: 🐛 [平台/Adapter] 历史消息 API 返回的文件消息缺少 CDN 下载链接，龙虾无法获取历史文件
items.content:
## 问题描述

龙虾通过历史消息 API 拉取群聊历史时，文件类型的消息只返回了文件名（如 `公示结果报告.doc`、`申报人个人承诺书.docx`），没有返回 CDN 下载链接，导致龙虾无法下载和处理这些历史文件。

## 复现步骤

1. 用户在群聊中发送了文件（doc/docx 等）
2. 龙虾通过历史消息 API 查询该群的历史消息
3. API 返回了文件消息，但 payload 中只有文件名，没有下载 URL

## 期望行为

历史消息 API 返回的文件消息 payload 中应包含完整的 CDN 下载链接（与实时收到文件消息时一致），龙虾可以直接下载文件内容。

## 实际行为

只返回文件名等元信息，无下载链接，龙虾无法获取文件。

## 影响

龙虾在需要回溯历史文件时完全无法工作（如用户让龙虾找之前发过的文件、分析历史附件等场景）。

## 原始来源

DMWork group:Bug反馈群 user:苏安炀 (uid:a83cdc88602c41e286fe0f77474cebc1)
items.author: lejunliu

## OpenClaw 项目深度报告

**OpenClaw 项目动态日报（2026-04-16）**

---

### 1. **今日速览**  
OpenClaw 在 2026-04-16 保持高度活跃状态，过去 24 小时内处理了 500 条 Issues 和 500 条 PRs，其中 366 个 Issue 被关闭、162 个 PR 已合并，表明社区修复效率较高。新版本 v2026.4.15-beta.1 发布，重点增强 OAuth 模型认证状态监控能力。整体项目处于稳定迭代期，但存在多个影响用户体验的回归问题待解决。

---

### 2. **版本发布**  
**v2026.4.15-beta.1**  
本次 Beta 版主要更新聚焦于控制界面（Control UI）的模型认证可视化：新增 **Model Auth Status Card**，实时展示 OAuth token 健康状态及提供商速率限制压力，并在令牌即将过期或已失效时提供醒目告警。该功能基于新引入的 `models.authStatus` 网关方法实现，该方法自动剥离凭证并对结果缓存 60 秒以提升安全性与性能。

> ✅ 无破坏性变更；无需迁移操作。

---

### 3. **项目进展**  
今日合并/关闭的关键 PR 包括：
- **[PR #67588]**：扩展 `/readyz` 端点以暴露 `bootCompleted` 状态，确保 onboarding trigger 在 gateway 启动完成后才触发，避免消息丢失（[链接](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/67588)）。
- **[PR #67077]**：修复 Windows 下并发配置热重载导致的 `auth-profiles.json` 只读属性冲突问题，将后处理保存设为非致命错误（[链接](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/67077)）。
- **[PR #67159]**：统一 OpenAI Codex 的 baseUrl 规范化逻辑，支持旧版路径 `/backend-api/v1`（[链接](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/67159)）。
- **[PR #67509]**：CLI 入口添加 root 用户保护机制，防止因以 root 运行导致 state 目录污染（[链接](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/67509)）。

以上改进显著提升了系统健壮性与跨平台兼容性。

---

items.title: Add OpenClaw/Xiaolongxia pull-only platform integration: backend endpoints, auth, service and front-end token flow
items.content:
### Motivation
- Provide a pull-only platform integration (OpenClaw / 小龙虾) so remote adapters can poll events and push messages. 
- Offer a scoped platform token issuance endpoint so authenticated users can generate tokens from the Settings UI and copy them into external services. 
- Secure platform APIs with a middleware that supports both a static API key and user-scoped JWT tokens with scope enforcement.

### Description
- Add backend platform auth middleware `platformAuth.ts` with `authenticatePlatformApiKey`, `issuePlatformAccessToken`, and `requirePlatformScope` to validate static keys and signed JWTs and expose plugin/scopes on requests. 
- Implement platform routes in `routes/platform.ts` and wire them under `/api/v1/platform` in `app.ts`, exposing endpoints for listing events (`GET /events`), ACKing events (`POST /events/ack`), creating/patching messages (`POST /messages`, `PATCH /messages/:messageId`), and resolving conversations (`GET /conversations/resolve`). 
- Add `services/platformIntegrationService.ts` that implements event listing, ack handling (idempotent), message creation/patching, and conversation lookup backed by the DB layer. 
- Add a platform token issuance endpoint `GET /api/config/platform-token` in `routes/config.ts` that returns a token bundle (`token`, `pluginId`, `scopes`, `baseUrl`, `expiresIn`). 
- Frontend: add `LlmConfigService.fetchPlatformToken` and `PlatformTokenBundle`, extend `ModelSettingsScreen` to request, display and let users copy the token and metadata, and improve `copyToClipboard` snackbar handling. 
- Update and add automated tests and documentation: update `model_settings_screen_test.dart` to exercise the UI token flow, add `platform.test.ts` for platform route auth/ACK semantics, and update `feature_map.yaml`, `logic_map.yaml`, and add a plan `docs/plans/2026-04-16-05-00-UTC-openclaw-gap-audit-and-token-chain.md`.

### Testing
- Ran the Flutter widget tests in `apps/mobile_chat_app/test/model_settings_screen_test.dar

items.title: 🐛 [Adapter] 群组级别 requireMention 配置不生效，插件只读取账号级别配置
items.content:
## 问题描述

在 `openclaw.json` 中按群组设置 `requireMention: true`（如 `groups.864cc.requireMention`），不生效。

## 根因

插件代码第 967 行：
```js
const requireMention = account.config.requireMention !== false;
```

只读取了 `account.config.requireMention`（账号级别），没有检查 `groups.[channelId].requireMention`（群组级别）。群组级别的配置根本没有被合并进来。

## 修复方案

在第 967 行改为同时检查群组级别配置，群组级别优先于账号级别：
```js
const groupConfig = account.config.groups?.[channelId];
const requireMention = groupConfig?.requireMention !== undefined
    ? groupConfig.requireMention
    : (account.config.requireMention !== false);
```

## 原始来源

items.title: REM topic extraction produces role-tag / boilerplate "themes" on chat transcripts (frequency-based signal is structurally unsuited to chat corpus)
items.content:
## Summary

On a real personal-assistant chat deployment (heavy Telegram/BlueBubbles/WhatsApp traffic + cron agent turns), REM topic extraction consistently promotes **transcript structural words** (`user`, `assistant`, role tags, boilerplate replies like "好的" / "HEARTBEAT_OK") as top themes, with `confidence=1.00` and `recalls=0`, even after input-layer sanitization is in place.

## Evidence

Local short-term-recall snapshot contains staged candidates like:

```
Theme: `assistant` kept surfacing across 1559 memories.  confidence: 1.00
Theme: `user` kept surfacing across ...  confidence: 1.00
status: staged  recalls: 0
```

This is after all current input cleaning landed:
- #66548 (strip inbound envelopes from user turns) — mine, merged
- #67315 (skip dreaming narrative transcripts before bootstrap records land)
- Local patch to skip cron session transcripts during ingestion

So the candidates above are what REM produces on **clean** input.

## Why stopwords won't fix it

Filtering `user`/`assistant`/role-tag tokens would only hand the crown to the next high-frequency class: polite acknowledgments (`好的`, `明白了`, "ok"), system-prompt structural words, or in gamified cron channels, game-state tokens (`繁荣`, `Lv2`). A chat transcript's token-frequency distribution is dominated by conversational scaffolding, not by topical content.

## Root cause (hypothesis)

REM's current signal — pick high-frequency tokens/phrases across session corpus as "themes that kept surfacing" — is a **structural mismatch** with conversational data. It works on document corpora (TF-IDF assumptions hold) but not on chat, where the same 10–30 scaffold words dominate every session regardless of topic.

## Suggested directions (not exhaustive)

1. **Replace frequency signal with LLM daily reflection**: once/day, hand the transcript to an LLM and ask for 3–5 concrete insight bullets. This is effectively what ad-hoc agent memory writes already do successfully (daily briefing cron, explicit \`memory_ad

items.title: Welcome @fang-agent — ugig applicant onramp
items.content:
Welcome, @fang-agent — saw your application on ugig for the "RustChain Open Bounties" listing. Thanks for reaching out.

**Two things to sync on before you pick a bounty:**

1. **Work happens here, not on ugig.** ugig is just where we post listings. The actual board, code review, and payouts all run through this GitHub repo. You don't need a ugig contract set up — claim a bounty directly here and we process it on delivery.

2. **We pay in RTC, not USD or fiat.** RTC is RustChain's native token, $0.10 USD reference rate. If you were expecting a USD-denominated gig, this isn't that. But if you're happy with RTC (and you should be — the payouts compound, and about 210+ contributors have earned ~18K RTC so far), welcome.

I wrote a full onramp at [GIG_APPLICANTS.md](./GIG_APPLICANTS.md) that walks through the 4-step flow (pick → claim → ship → get paid).

**For you specifically**, given your cover letter says you specialize in Python automation and data engineering:

- [#2861](https://github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties/issues/2861) — **50 RTC** — Build an autonomous bounty-claim agent (meta: an agent that claims bounties is the bounty). You're literally an OpenClaw agent, so this is in your wheelhouse.
- [#747](https://github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties/issues/747) — **50–75 RTC** — Build the auto-verify star/follow bot. Excel/Sheets-to-Python migration skills transfer directly to "scrape GitHub API, cross-reference claims, post payout receipts."
- [#36](https://github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties/issues/36) — Python SDK bounty. Partially claimed but still open for add-ons.
- [#2864](https://github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties/issues/2864) — **20 RTC** — GitHub Action that auto-awards RTC on PR merge. Python + YAML.

Your OpenClaw background is a fit here. We have several other OpenClaw agents in the contributor base (@fengqiankun6-sudo, @kolatrerionpu-hash, @chienvon, others) — you'd be in good company.

Pick one, post a claim comment on the issue, ship,
items.author: Scottcjn

items.title: fix(cnp): remove egress rules from all CNPs (Cilium 1.18.3 ClusterIP breakage)
items.content:
## Summary

- **Root cause**: Cilium 1.18.3 bug: `egress: [{}]` in a namespace-level CNP combined with the cluster CCNP (`enableDefaultDeny: false`) causes pods to lose ClusterIP connectivity (i/o timeout to `10.96.0.1:443`). All Kubernetes informers break → kyverno, cert-manager, policy-reporter, etc. crash
- **Fix**: Remove `egress` sections from all 71 namespace-level CNPs. Since CCNPs use `enableDefaultDeny: {egress: false}`, pods have open egress by default without explicit egress rules
- Also fixes kyverno webhook CNPs: add `host` + `remote-node` entities (Talos kube-apiserver uses `hostNetwork: true`, doesn't match the `kube-apiserver` Cilium entity alone)

## Closes

Part of the CNP rollout from #2979. Discovered after deploying CNPs that had `egress: [{}]`.

## Test plan

- [ ] kyverno admission controller starts and reaches API server (no i/o timeout)
- [ ] cert-manager-cainjector recovers
- [ ] policy-reporter recovers
- [ ] ArgoCD syncs successfully

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

items.title: 📢 OpenClaw 新版本发布: v2026.4.15
items.content:
## 🆕 检测到 OpenClaw 新版本

**当前教程版本**: v2026.4.11  
**最新官方版本**: v2026.4.15

### 📝 Release Notes

### Changes

- Anthropic/models: default Anthropic selections, `opus` aliases, Claude CLI defaults, and bundled image understanding to Claude Opus 4.7.
- Google/TTS: add Gemini text-to-speech support to the bundled `google` plugin, including provider registration, voice selection, WAV reply output, PCM telephony output, and setup/docs guidance. (#67515) Thanks @barronlroth.
- Control UI/Overview: add a Model Auth status card showing OAuth token health and provider rate-limit pressure at a glance, with attention callouts when OAuth tokens are expiring or expired. Backed by a new `models.authStatus` gateway method that strips credentials and caches for 60s. (#66211) Thanks @omarshahine.
- Memory/LanceDB: add cloud storage support to `memory-lancedb` so durable memory indexes can run on remote object storage instead of local disk only. (#63502) Thanks @rugvedS07.
- GitHub Copilot/memory search: add a GitHub Copilot embedding provider for memory search, and expose a dedicated Copilot embedding host helper so plugins can reuse the transport while honoring remote overrides, token refresh, and safer payload validation. (#61718) Thanks @feiskyer and @vincentkoc.
- Agents/local models: add experimental `agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean: true` to drop heavyweight default tools like `browser`, `cron`, and `message`, reducing prompt size for weaker local-model setups without changing the normal path. (#66495) Thanks @ImLukeF.
- Packaging/plugins: localize bundled plugin runtime deps to their owning extensions, trim the published docs payload, and tighten install/package-manager guardrails so published builds stay leaner and core stops carrying extension-owned runtime baggage. (#67099) Thanks @vincentkoc.
- QA/Matrix: split Matrix live QA into a source-linked `qa-matrix` runner and keep repo-private `qa-*` surfaces out of packaged and published builds. (#66723) Thanks @gumade
items.author: github-actions[bot]

items.title: feat: pbrain install-skills — auto-register skills in Claude Code / Cursor / Windsurf
items.content:
## Summary

- Close the biggest install-friction gap: PBrain's 26 fat-markdown skills now auto-register in Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf via `pbrain install-skills`, which symlinks every \`skills/<name>/SKILL.md\` into each client's discovery directory.
- Auto-runs on \`pbrain init\` (prompted, default yes) and \`pbrain upgrade\` (silent), so strangers cloning the repo get working skills in their editor with zero manual steps, and existing users pick up newly-added skills on upgrade.
- Collisions are never silently overwritten — foreign symlinks / files are skipped with exit 2, real directories refuse even \`--force\`. \`pbrain install-skills uninstall\` only removes links pointing back into the repo.
- \`pbrain doctor\` gains a \`skill_symlinks\` check (broken / partial / shadowed).

## Context

Before this PR, \`INSTALL_FOR_AGENTS.md\` Step 5 said "read \`skills/RESOLVER.md\` and save it to memory" — which works for dedicated agent platforms (OpenClaw, Hermes) that read the skills dir directly, but does **not** work for the IDE clients PBrain's README explicitly lists as supported. A Claude Code user ended up with a CLI, MCP server, and brain — but no skill ever auto-fired because the skill files were invisible to Claude Code's skill discovery. This is an upstream gap inherited from GBrain (which targets dedicated agent platforms).

## Design notes

- **Symlinks, not copies** — \`git pull\` updates propagate automatically. No desync state.
- **Symlinks only when the client exists** — probes \`~/.claude\`, \`~/.cursor\`, \`~/.windsurf\`. Missing clients are skipped silently.
- **Collision policy** is the careful part. Rule 4 in the plan: if a target is already our symlink, skip (idempotent); if it's a foreign symlink or real file, warn + skip + exit 2; if it's a real directory, refuse even under \`--force\` (almost certainly owned by another plugin with multiple files inside).
- **Scope**: \`--scope user\` (default, \`~/.<client>/skills\`) vs \`--scope project\

items.author: RemindD

items.title: feat(memory): knowledge-experience decoupling
items.content:
## Summary

Implements the knowledge / experience decoupling pattern from *Graph-based Agent Memory: Taxonomy, Techniques, and Applications* ([arxiv:2602.05665](https://arxiv.org/html/2602.05665v1) §III-C, §V-E).

- **Knowledge** — static, objective reference data (`profile` / `preferences` / `entities` / `patterns`)
- **Experience** — trajectory of interactions and outcomes (`events` / `cases`)

The plugin already did this *partially* on the write side (`profile` always-merges, `events`/`cases` append-only). This PR threads the same split through decay and retrieval without splitting storage.

## What this changes

| Layer | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| Classification | implicit in merge rules | `classifyMemoryType()` in `src/memory-categories.ts`, stored as `metadata.memory_type` with lazy backfill on read |
| Decay | β per tier only | Weibull half-life now multiplied by `knowledgeHalfLifeMultiplier` (default **3.0**) or `experienceHalfLifeMultiplier` (default **0.7**) per memory |
| Intent | category-only routing | `analyzeIntent` now returns a `memoryType` hint; new "experience" rule catches `上次` / `之前` / `last time` / `when we ...` |
| Retrieval | `applyCategoryBoost` | same, then `applyMemoryTypeBoost` (1.15x) for type-matched hits in auto-recall |
| Recall tool | `query / scope / category` | adds `type: "knowledge" \| "experience" \| "both"` post-filter |
| Config | decay + tier knobs | two new `decay.*Multiplier` keys in `openclaw.plugin.json` schema |

Decoupling sits at the **semantic layer**, not the storage layer — no new tables, same LanceDB `memories`, same scopes. Matches this plugin's existing pattern of extending via JSON `metadata` rather than schema changes.

## Test plan

- [x] New `test/knowledge-experience-decoupling.test.mjs` — 18 subtests covering classifier mapping, `parseSmartMetadata` backfill, per-type half-life, intent routing (EN + 中文), `applyMemoryTypeBoost`
- [x] Registered under `core-regression` in `scripts/ci-test-manifest.mjs`

**📅 AI Agent & Skills 每日情报简报 (2026-04-17)**

**🚀 今日头条**
1. **Anthropic 发布 Claude Opus 4.7**：最新模型上线，进一步强化复杂任务处理能力，成为 Agent 核心驱动。
2. **OpenClaw 更新 v2026.4.15**：框架迅速适配 Opus 4.7，默认捆绑图像理解能力并优化 CLI 配置。
3. **OpenAI 推出 Codex for Almost Everything**：代码生成能力泛化，旨在覆盖几乎所有开发场景。
4. **Google 发布 Android CLI Agent 支持**：支持任意 Agent 构建 Android 应用，效率提升 3 倍。
5. **AI 驱动硬件黑客臂项目曝光**：开发者利用旧摄像头与 CNC 机器构建自主物理探测臂。

**📦 GitHub 亮点项目**
*   **[openclaw/openclaw](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.15)**: **v2026.4.15 发布**。更新内容包括默认 Anthropic 模型选择、`opus` 别名支持、Claude CLI 默认值标准化，以及为 Claude Opus 4.7 捆绑图像理解功能。
*   **[gainsec/autoprober](https://github.com/gainsec/autoprober)**: **AI 硬件探测臂**。结合 duct tape、旧摄像头和 CNC 机器，实现 AI 驱动的硬件自动化测试与交互。
*   **[langchain-ai/langgraph](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph/releases/tag/cli%3D%3D0.4.22)**: **cli==0.4.22 发布**。主要更新包括 CLI 版本提升、开始追踪 CLI 部署源以及 LangSmith 依赖 bump。

**📚 研究进展**
*   **硬件仿真验证闭环**: 展示了通过 Claude Code 连接 Spice 仿真、示波器硬件并进行自动验证的技术路径，标志着 AI 在电子工程领域的深度介入。
*   **编程体验优化**: 社区探索利用 Tree-sitter 提升 R 语言编程体验，为数据科学 Agent 提供更精准的代码解析基础。

**🛠️ 技术动态**
*   **OpenClaw 技能更新**: 新版本强化了对 Anthropic 模型的支持，特别是针对 Opus 4.7 的图像理解能力进行了默认捆绑，降低了开发者集成门槛。
*   **MCP 协议落地**: 出现基于 LeCroy 示波器的 MCP 服务演示，实现了从软件仿真 (Spice) 到硬件验证 (Oscilloscope) 的协议打通。
*   **Agent Skills 扩展**: Android CLI 工具链正式支持接入任意 Agent，标志着移动端开发流程开始全面 Agent 化。

**🇨🇳 中文社区**

items.title: Sync VM config into defaults; migrate home docs to data disk
items.content:
## Summary

- Pulled the live VM's main config + exec-approvals and merged the non-secret fields back into `vm-runtime/defaults/`. Per-VM tokens (`gateway.auth.token`, MCP server credentials, exec-approvals socket token) and runtime metadata (`meta`, `wizard`, `plugins.installs`, per-install UUIDs) were stripped before write.
- New `vm-runtime/updates/011-home-claude-md-symlink.sh`: idempotent migration that puts `~/CLAUDE.md` on the data disk at `~/workspace/CLAUDE.md` (bind mount → `/mnt/claw-data/workspace/CLAUDE.md`) and symlinks home to it. Handles fresh VMs (seeded default), existing VMs (regular file migration), and already-linked VMs. Divergent home copies get timestamped `.bak` before the data-disk copy wins.
- Seeded the same doc into `vm-runtime/defaults/workspace/CLAUDE.md` so fresh fleet members receive it on first boot.

items.title: fix(website): correct false claims and stale numbers in live docs
items.content:
## Summary

The live docs site had several false claims, stale numbers, and unverifiable marketing absolutes. This PR fixes them without changing any product behavior — it's docs/copy only.

### Factual fixes
- **Landing page terminal demo** showed a `mempalace remember` command that doesn't exist in [cli.py](mempalace/cli.py). Replaced with real `mempalace mine ./notes`.
- **MCP tool count: 19 → 29** across [mcp-integration.md](website/guide/mcp-integration.md), [claude-code.md](website/guide/claude-code.md), [openclaw.md](website/guide/openclaw.md), and [modules.md](website/reference/modules.md). Verified by counting `"mempalace_*"` entries in the `TOOLS` dict of [mcp_server.py](mempalace/mcp_server.py).
- **Tool Overview in [mcp-integration.md](website/guide/mcp-integration.md)** was missing entire categories. Added Drawers (`get_drawer`, `list_drawers`, `update_drawer`), Tunnels (`create_tunnel`, `list_tunnels`, `delete_tunnel`, `follow_tunnels`), and System (`hook_settings`, `memories_filed_away`, `reconnect`).
- **Wake-up token range `~170–900` → `~600–900`** in [cli.md](website/reference/cli.md), [api-reference.md](website/reference/api-reference.md), and [python-api.md](website/reference/python-api.md) to match the CLI help text in [cli.py](mempalace/cli.py) and the concept doc in [memory-stack.md](website/concepts/memory-stack.md).
- **Gemini CLI command** in [gemini-cli.md](website/guide/gemini-cli.md) had ambiguous flag order — `--scope user` trailing after `-m mempalace.mcp_server` could be parsed as a module arg. Moved `--scope user` before the target name and added the standard `--` separator.

### Marketing softening (unverifiable absolutes)
- "forever available. One hundred percent recall by design." → "permanent. Designed for total recall."
- "retrieved <50 ms" → "retrieved instantly" (no published latency benchmark backed the 50 ms figure)
- "Ninety-plus percent compression... One hundred percent fidelity" → "Dense compression... Full fidelity"

items.title: fix(cal-mcp): raw DTSTART extraction for recurring TZID (v0.7.2)
items.content:
## Summary
- Fixes recurring-event TZID drift where `node-ical` resolves `TZID` params inconsistently across Node builds / container tzdata, shifting occurrences by the resolution delta.
- Adds `extractDtstartWallClockFromIcs()` — reads wall-clock time-of-day + TZID directly from the raw ICS `DTSTART` line (with RFC 5545 unfolding + UID-keyed VEVENT match). Recurrence expansion now uses this deterministic source instead of `vevent.start`.
- Falls back to the existing path when no `TZID` param is present.
- Bumps cal-mcp to **v0.7.2**.

## Bug reproduced
Inside `openclaw-gateway` container: "Kathy / Miguel monthly chisme" (`TZID=America/Los_Angeles`) returned as `2026-04-17T06:00:00-05:00` (6 AM CT) instead of the correct `11:00 CT`. Non-recurring events (Paragon Pest Control, Rosemount Rodeo) were unaffected — isolating the bug to the recurrence-expansion path's reliance on node-ical's parsed `Date`.

## Test plan
- [x] New: 6 unit tests for TZID extraction (TZID param, UTC DTSTART, multi-VEVENT UID match, missing UID, RFC 5545 line folding, end-to-end regression for the Kathy chisme case)
- [x] `npx vitest run` in cal-mcp: **141/141 passing**
- [x] `npm run typecheck` clean across workspace
- [ ] After merge, tag `cal-mcp/v0.7.2` to trigger publish workflow
- [ ] Restart Edith so mcporter fetches the new version; re-run `list_events` for 2026-04-17 and confirm Kathy chisme resolves to `11:00 CT`

items.author: liuzhq1986

items.title: [FEATURE] Claude Code multi device, chat and progress sync system
items.content:
### Preflight Checklist

- [x] I have searched [existing requests](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20label%3Aenhancement) and this feature hasn't been requested yet
- [x] This is a single feature request (not multiple features)

### Problem Statement

Claude Code is tied to a specific terminal process on a specific machine. For users who work across multiple devices (desktop, laptop, phone, home server, cloud envs), there's no unified way to see what's running where, pick up work on a different device, or send a prompt to the right machine.

I run three Windows machines (desktop, laptop, mini PC gateway hub) plus a phone, and I move between them constantly through a typical day. Right now my Claude Code experience is fragmented: each device is its own island, each session is locked to the terminal it was started in, and moving context between them means lossy copy-paste or heavyweight manual setup.

Remote Control is a partial bridge but it's per-session, manually started, one-viewer-at-a-time, requires a terminal staying open, and doesn't scale to "I have three machines all running Claude Code and I want one surface to work with all of them". It feels like a workaround, not the product.

The core missing abstraction is device as a first-class identity. I think about "my mini PC" and "my laptop", not about "session 48293". Everything in Claude Code's current model is session-first, which doesn't match how users actually use multiple machines.

### Proposed Solution

### The chat is the shared object. The device is the executor. Sync the object.
Each device registers to the account as a node. Chats live on the device where they were created because that's where the filesystem, MCP servers, and execution context live. A lightweight sync client on each device pushes chat state diffs to Anthropic's relay, which mirrors them out to any connected viewer.

**The Claude app (mobile, web, desktop) shows:**
- A list of my nodes, each with a statu

items.title: Implement Phase 1 sovereign runtime spine
items.content:
## Objective
Implement Phase 1 of the sovereign runtime spine defined by the repo docs.

This issue is the first runtime realization of the original plan. It is a **merge + upgrade** step, not a rewrite, and it must preserve the sovereign repo-centered governor.

## Authoritative docs
Read and implement against these first:
- `docs/BUILD_ORDER.md`
- `docs/SOURCE_SYSTEM_BLUEPRINT.md`
- `docs/SCHEMA_CONTRACTS.md`
- `docs/RISK_TAXONOMY.md`
- `docs/MEMORY_WRITE_POLICY.md`
- `docs/EXECUTION_RECEIPTS.md`
- `docs/reference/DONOR_ADOPTION_MATRIX.md`
- `docs/build/PHASE_1_SOVEREIGN_RUNTIME_SPINE.md`
- `docs/build/PHASE_1_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA.md`

## Scope
Implement only the Phase 1 runtime spine:

### 1. Source router / authority resolver
- classify tasks into architecture / runtime / build / reference / external
- resolve preferred source tier
- escalate on source conflict
- fail closed on unresolved high-impact source conflict
- produce source attribution inputs

Target files:
- `app/orchestrator/task_classifier.py`
- `app/orchestrator/context_loader.py`
- `app/orchestrator/source_router.py`
- `app/schemas/task.py`
- `app/schemas/source_resolution.py`

### 2. Governor state machine
- explicit lifecycle: intake, context_load, plan, authorize, execute, observe, verify, persist, respond, fail_closed
- explicit retry / replan transitions
- inspectable run state after failure

Target files:
- `app/orchestrator/state_machine.py`
- `app/orchestrator/loop_manager.py`
- `app/orchestrator/execution_planner.py`
- `app/schemas/execution_graph.py`

### 3. Risk + approval service
- classify every planned step by risk
- enforce at least: `READ_ONLY`, `LOCAL_WRITE`, `LOCAL_EXECUTION`, `EXTERNAL_API_CALL`, `DATABASE_MUTATION`, `BROWSER_AUTOMATION`, `CREDENTIAL_USE`, `IRREVERSIBLE`
- no builder/tool bypass

- `app/policy/risk_engine.py`
- `app/policy/approval_policy.py`
- `app/policy/action_constraints.py`
- `app/orchestrator/approvals.py`
- `app/schemas/approval_request.py`
